Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given an event log of an insurance claims process with 5 cases 2001 2005 We need to 1 Identify cases with significantly longer duration 2 Analyze attributes Resource Region Complexity to find root causes 3 Propose explanations and suggestions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First compute the total duration lead time for each case from the first event Submit Claim to the last event Close Claim Well use the timestamps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 End 2024 04 01 10 30 Duration 1 hour 30 minutes 1 5 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 End 2024 04 02 11 00 Duration From 09 05 on 04 01 to 11 00 on 04 02 Thats 24 hours 1 hour 55 minutes total 25 hours 55 minutes 25 917 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 End 2024 04 03 09 30 Duration From 09 10 on 04 01 to 09 30 on 04 03 is 48 hours 20 minutes 48 33 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 End 2024 04 01 10 45 Duration 1 hour 25 minutes 1 417 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 End 2024 04 04 14 30 Duration total 77 hours 5 minutes 77 083 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Clearly cases 2003 and 2005 are significantly longer 48 and 77 hours compared to 2001 and 2004 about 1 5 hours and 2002 about 26 hours But note that 2002 is also longer than 2001 and 2004 So we consider 2002 2003 2005 as having longer durations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now we need to analyze the attributes Resource Region Complexity

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Region A Complexity Low Resources CSR Jane Adjuster Mike Manager Ann Finance Alan CSR Jane no Request Additional Documents So it went straight from Evaluate to Approve

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Region B Complexity Medium Resources CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Adjuster Lisa Manager Ann Finance Carl CSR Paul Had one Request Additional Documents event

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Region A Complexity High Resources CSR Jane Adjuster Mike Adjuster Mike Adjuster Mike Manager Bill Finance Alan CSR Jane Had two Request Additional Documents events both by Adjuster Mike

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 Region B Complexity Low Resources CSR Mary Adjuster Lisa Manager Ann Finance Carl CSR Mary No Request Additional Documents

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Region B Complexity High Resources CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Adjuster Lisa Adjuster Lisa Manager Bill Finance Carl CSR Paul Had three Request Additional Documents events all by Adjuster Lisa

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The main observation is that the cases with Request Additional Documents have longer durations Specifically Case 2001 Low no extra docs duration 1 5 hours Case 2004 Low no extra docs duration 1 4 hours Case 2002 Medium one extra docs duration about 26 hours Case 2003 High two extra docs duration about 48 hours Case 2005 High three extra docs duration about 77 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So complexity correlates with the number of additional document requests Low complexity no extra docs Medium one High two or three And the total duration increases with the number of extra docs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In case 2002 after Request Additional Documents at 14 00 on 04 01 the next event is Approve Claim at 10 00 on 04 02 about 20 hours later Similarly in case 2003 the two requests are on 04 01 at 11 00 and 17 00 and the approval is on 04 02 at 16 00 about 23 hours after the last request In case 2005 three requests on 04 01 04 02 04 03 and approval on 04 04 at 10 00 about 19 hours after the last request

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the additional document requests cause delays because the claim is held until the documents are received and processed The more requests the longer the delay

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Region A Cases 2001 Low fast and 2003 High slow Region B Cases 2002 Medium slow 2004 Low fast 2005 High slow So within each region complexity seems to be the main driver

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region itself is not a cause Resource The adjusters are different Adjuster Mike handles Region A claims Adjuster Lisa handles Region B claims But both adjusters handle low complexity quickly and high complexity slowly

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe Adjuster Lisa tends to request more documents than Adjuster Mike But we have only one high complexity case per adjuster so its hard to say

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Possibly high complexity claims require approval from a more senior manager Manager Bill which might also cause delays

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The approval times for high complexity are around 19 23 hours after last request which might be normal for that manager

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the main root cause appears to be the complexity of the claim leading to requests for additional documents which introduce waiting periods The more complex the claim the more documents are requested leading to longer total duration

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 fast Submit to Evaluate 30 min Evaluate to Approve 30 min Approve to Pay 15 min Pay to Close 15 min

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 fast Submit to Evaluate 15 min Evaluate to Approve 25 min Approve to Pay 30 min Pay to Close 15 min So for low complexity steps are very fast

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 medium Submit to Evaluate 40 min Evaluate to Request Additional Documents 4 hours 15 min Request Additional Documents to Approve 20 hours Approve to Pay 45 min Pay to Close 15 min So the major delays are Evaluate to Request and Request to Approve

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 high Submit to Evaluate 30 min Evaluate to First Request Additional Documents 1 hour 20 min First Request to Second Request Additional Documents 6 hours Second Request to Approve 23 hours Approve to Pay 17 hours Pay to Close 30 min

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The delays between requests may mean waiting for first set of documents and then realizing more are needed Also approval to payment took 17 hours possibly due to overnight processing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 high Submit to Evaluate 25 min Evaluate to First Request Additional Documents 1 hour 40 min First Request to Second Request Additional Documents 29 5 hours Second Request to Third Request Additional Documents 22 hours Third Request to Approve 19 hours Approve to Pay 4 hours Pay to Close 30 min

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: These likely represent the time taken by the customer to provide the documents The more requests the longer the process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the root cause is clearly the complexity leading to multiple document requests which introduce significant waiting times

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region does not seem to be a direct cause Resource CSR different CSRs but they seem to perform similarly Adjusters both handle claims but the difference in number of document requests might be due to claim complexity rather than the adjuster

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Its possible that Adjuster Lisa is more thorough or the claims she handles require more documentation But we do not have enough data

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main factor is claim complexity The cases with high complexity 2003 2005 and also medium complexity 2002 have longer durations The root cause is the need for additional documents which depends on the complexity of the claim

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: High complexity claims likely involve more stringent verification requiring more documentation from the claimant The process of requesting waiting for and reviewing documents adds significant waiting time Medium complexity claims may also require some documentation but less than high Low complexity claims are straightforward and can be processed quickly without extra documentation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Suggestions For high complexity claims consider upfront documentation requirements to reduce back and forth Provide a checklist of required documents at submission Implement automated reminders to claimants when documents are pending Train adjusters to request all necessary documents in one go if possible Consider parallel processing Monitor adjusters and managers for bottlenecks Use predictive analytics to identify likely high complexity claims early and allocate resources accordingly
